task_summary: Panel cohort cell: p1_compliance asks composer to build the batch_aggregation pipeline shape. Generated from panel-broad-2026-05-09.yaml on the panel-broad cohort.
opening_prompt:
I need to set up a workflow to process some transaction data we've received, and I want to make sure we're handling it correctly from a controls testing perspective. We have a CSV file with transaction records — each entry has an ID, date, category, amount, and region — and depending on how the data arrives, there could be some entries that don't fit our expected structure. Those should be flagged and set aside rather than processed further, just to be safe.

What I'm really after is a summary report that groups transactions by category and gives us the totals, the count, and the average amount per category. We need this rolled up in batches — so once we've accumulated five transactions, we should calculate the summary for that batch and move it to an output file. The idea is that we can then forward these summaries to second-line review without having to manually recalculate anything, which reduces the risk of error.

I should mention: we'll need to keep an evidentiary record of what was processed and what was discarded, in case we're audited on this later. I'm assuming the system keeps some kind of audit log automatically? And ideally the final output should be in a format I can open in Excel and hand off to my colleague. Could we set this up so that any entries that don't meet our data quality standards are withdrawn from the main flow, and the clean summaries end up in a separate file ready for review?

build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Lower-case substring match against final assistant content. The skill (anti-passivity section) explicitly forbids these phrases — any hit is RED. Standard list shared with the convergence suite.
red_criteria.build_failure_note: These strings are server-injected by service.py:_build_runtime_preflight_message() when the model declares completion but the pipeline fails preflight. Their presence is definitive evidence the model failed to converge.
